question_text: The area of the triangle on the complex plane formed by the complex numbers z, – iz and z + iz is: 2 2 2 z 2 2025-26
answer_key: (C), Let z = x + iy. Then – iz = y – ix. Therefore,
answer_explanation: z + iz = (x – y) + i (x + y) 2 1 2 z Required area of the triangle = (x + y2 ) = 2 2

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The equation z +1 − i = z −1+ i represents a
answer_explanation: ⇒ z − (−1 + i ) = z − (1 − i ) ⇒ PA = PB, where A denotes the point (–1, 1), B denotes the point (1, –1) and P denotes the point (x, y) ⇒ z lies on the perpendicular bisector of the line joining A and B and perpendicular bisector is a straight line. 2

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Number of solutions of the equation z2 + z = 0 is 2
answer_explanation: ⇒ x2 – y2 + i2xy + x2 + y2 = 0 ⇒ 2x2 + i2xy = 0 2x (x + iy) = 0 ⇒ x = 0 or x + iy = 0 (not possible) Therefore, x = 0 and z ≠ 0 So y can have any real value. Hence infinitely many solutions. π π
provenance_excerpt: Number of solutions of the equation z2 + z = 0 is 2
